pr_text:
## Title: Fix integer overflow in UE RX resynchronization loops

### Summary
The UE RX resynchronization logic in `syncInFrame()` uses a 32-bit `int` for the loop counter when iterating over sample offsets. When `rx_offset` (an `openair0_timestamp`, typically 64-bit) exceeds INT_MAX, storing it in a 32-bit integer causes overflow, leading to incorrect loop behavior and potential system instability. This affects both IQ player/recorder modes and normal operation paths. I promote the loop variable
